top_items.title: High-Fidelity Synthetic Transmission Electron Microscopy Image Generation Using Diffusion Probabilistic Models for Data-Limited Semiconductor Metrology

definition: Diffusion models are a class of generative models that produce images, audio, or other data by learning to reverse a gradual noising process, starting from random noise and iteratively denoising it into a coherent sample.
